articles.headline: Eight Police Personnel Killed in Bijapur Naxal Attack, Raises Internal Security Concerns
articles.explainer:
• A vehicle carrying police personnel was targeted by a landmine planted by Naxalites in Bijapur district, Chhattisgarh.
• Eight police officers and one civilian were killed in the ambush, highlighting the persistent challenge of Left-Wing Extremism (LWE).
• The incident occurred during a routine patrol, leading to a strong condemnation from central and state authorities.
• Security forces have launched a massive search operation in the region to apprehend the perpetrators.
• This attack underscores the need for enhanced intelligence, improved anti-Naxal strategies, and community engagement in affected areas.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
Role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Bijapur district is located in Chhattisgarh, a state significantly affected by LWE.
Landmines are a common tactic used by Naxalites in ambushes.
Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) like CRPF assist state police in anti-Naxal operations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to the persistence of Naxalism in regions like Chhattisgarh.
Discuss the effectiveness of current anti-Naxal strategies, including development initiatives and security operations.
Evaluate the challenges faced by security forces in LWE-affected areas and suggest measures for improving their operational capabilities and intelligence gathering.
Examine the role of state and central governments in coordinating efforts to tackle Left-Wing Extremism.
articles.imageDescription: Security personnel inspecting the site of a Naxal attack in a forested area of Bijapur, Chhattisgarh.
articles.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

articles.headline: Indian Coast Guard HAL Dhruv Helicopter Crashes in Porbandar, Three Crew Members Dead
articles.explainer:
• An advanced light helicopter (ALH) Dhruv of the Indian Coast Guard crashed during a training flight near Porbandar, Gujarat.
• All three crew members on board, including the pilot, co-pilot, and an observer, succumbed to their injuries.
• A court of inquiry has been ordered to investigate the cause of the accident, which occurred during a routine sortie.
• The HAL Dhruv is an indigenously developed utility helicopter, widely used by the Indian Armed Forces and Coast Guard.
• The incident raises concerns about aviation safety protocols and maintenance procedures for military aircraft.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: HAL Dhruv is manufactured by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).
The Indian Coast Guard is a multi-mission organization conducting maritime law enforcement and search and rescue operations.
Porbandar is a coastal city in Gujarat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of indigenization in India's defence sector and the role of HAL in achieving self-reliance.
Analyze the challenges in maintaining aviation safety standards for military aircraft in India and suggest measures for improvement.
Examine the role and responsibilities of the Indian Coast Guard in ensuring maritime security and safety along India's coastline.
articles.imageDescription: An Indian Coast Guard HAL Dhruv helicopter in flight over a coastal area.
articles.trivia: The HAL Dhruv has been exported to several countries, including Ecuador, Maldives, and Mauritius.

articles.headline: Nine Miners Trapped and Killed in Dima Hasao Coal Mine Flooding, Assam
articles.explainer:
• A tragic incident unfolded in Dima Hasao district, Assam, where nine miners were killed due to flooding in an illegal coal mine.
• The miners were reportedly trapped after heavy rains caused a sudden influx of water into the unscientific 'rat-hole' mine.
• Rescue operations faced significant challenges due to the remote location, hazardous conditions, and lack of proper mine safety equipment.
• The incident highlights the pervasive issue of illegal mining in resource-rich regions, often leading to environmental degradation and loss of life.
• Authorities have initiated an inquiry into the incident and pledged action against those responsible for operating the illegal mine.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Dima Hasao district is located in Assam, known for its hilly terrain and mineral resources.
Rat-hole mining is a controversial and dangerous method of coal extraction.
The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has previously banned rat-hole mining due to its environmental and safety hazards.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors that drive illegal mining activities in India, particularly in states like Assam.
Analyze the environmental consequences of unscientific mining practices, including land degradation, water pollution, and biodiversity loss.
Evaluate the challenges in enforcing mining regulations and ensuring worker safety in remote and LWE-affected areas.
Suggest comprehensive measures to curb illegal mining, including technological interventions, community involvement, and stringent legal frameworks.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers attempting to pump water out of a flooded mine shaft in a remote, hilly area.
articles.trivia: Rat-hole mining involves digging narrow tunnels, typically only large enough for one person to enter, to extract coal.

articles.headline: Eminent Kannada Novelist Na D'Souza Passes Away at 87
articles.explainer:
• Noted Kannada novelist and writer Na D'Souza, known for his significant contributions to children's literature and social themes, passed away at the age of 87.
• D'Souza was a recipient of numerous awards, including the Sahitya Akademi Award for his overall contribution to Kannada literature.
• His works often explored themes of human relationships, rural life, and the challenges faced by marginalized communities.
• He was particularly celebrated for his novel 'Dweepa' (The Island), which was adapted into a critically acclaimed film.
• His demise marks a significant loss for Kannada literature and the broader Indian literary landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Na D'Souza was a prominent figure in Kannada literature.
He was known for his novel 'Dweepa'.
The Sahitya Akademi Award is a literary honour in India given by the Sahitya Akademi, India's National Academy of Letters.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of regional literature in reflecting and shaping societal values and cultural identity in India.
Analyze the contributions of writers like Na D'Souza in promoting social awareness and addressing contemporary issues through their literary works.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for regional languages and literature in the digital age.
articles.imageDescription: A portrait of Na D'Souza, an elderly man with a thoughtful expression.
articles.trivia: Na D'Souza also served as a journalist and worked for various Kannada newspapers and magazines.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Stringent Environmental Clearance Norms for Infrastructure Projects
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today reaffirmed the necessity of stringent environmental clearance (EC) norms for all major infrastructure projects.
• The ruling came in a batch of petitions challenging relaxations in EC processes, emphasizing the 'polluter pays' principle and sustainable development.
• The court highlighted the irreversible damage caused by unchecked development and the importance of public participation in environmental impact assessments (EIAs).
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for balancing economic growth with environmental protection.
• It reinforces the judiciary's role in safeguarding ecological balance and holding project proponents accountable.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: Environmental Clearance (EC) is mandated under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' is a key concept in environmental law.
EIA is a tool used to assess the environmental consequences of a plan, policy, program, or project.
mainsPoints: Analyze the conflict between economic development and environmental conservation in India, citing relevant Supreme Court judgments.
Discuss the strengths and weaknesses of India's current environmental clearance process and suggest reforms for more effective implementation.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding environmental justice and promoting sustainable development goals in India.
Examine how public participation can be strengthened in the EIA process to ensure greater transparency and accountability.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi, symbolizing judicial authority.
articles.trivia: India's first major environmental legislation was the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974.

articles.headline: India's Q3 Economic Growth Projections Remain Robust Amid Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• Several leading economic agencies and the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) have released their projections for India's Q3 (October-December) economic growth, indicating continued robustness.
• Despite global economic slowdowns and geopolitical uncertainties, India's domestic consumption and investment are expected to drive growth.
• Manufacturing and services sectors show signs of resilience, contributing significantly to the GDP figures.
• Inflation remains a concern, but the RBI's monetary policy stance is seen as balancing growth and price stability.
• The government's focus on capital expenditure and structural reforms is credited with providing a stable foundation for economic expansion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Q3 in the Indian financial year refers to October-December.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is responsible for monetary policy in India.
GDP growth is a key indicator of economic health.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers of India's economic growth in recent quarters and identify potential challenges to sustaining this momentum.
Discuss the role of the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy in managing inflation and supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the impact of government's capital expenditure and structural reforms on India's economic resilience amidst global headwinds.
Examine the concept of 'inclusive growth' in the context of India's economic expansion and suggest measures to achieve it.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing an upward trending line representing India's economic growth on a stock market-like chart.
articles.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2027.

articles.headline: India Proposes New Framework for Indo-Pacific Economic Cooperation at Regional Summit
articles.explainer:
• India has put forth a comprehensive proposal for an enhanced Indo-Pacific Economic Cooperation Framework during a virtual regional summit.
• The framework aims to foster greater trade, investment, supply chain resilience, and digital economy collaboration among member nations.
• The proposal emphasizes principles of inclusivity, transparency, and respect for sovereignty, positioning India as a key player in regional architecture.
• Discussions focused on critical areas such as clean energy transition, infrastructure development, and maritime security.
• This initiative underscores India's growing strategic interest and leadership role in shaping the economic future of the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Indo-Pacific region is strategically important for global trade and security.
India is a key member of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) which focuses on the Indo-Pacific.
Supply chain resilience became a critical issue during the COVID-19 pandemic.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic rationale behind actively engaging in Indo-Pacific economic and security frameworks.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India in promoting a new economic cooperation framework in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate how India's 'Act East' policy integrates with its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Examine the role of multilateral economic groupings in shaping regional trade and investment landscapes.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map of the Indo-Pacific region, highlighting key trade routes and countries.
articles.trivia: The term 'Indo-Pacific' gained prominence in strategic discourse in the early 21st century, emphasizing the interconnectedness of the Indian and Pacific Oceans.

articles.headline: ISRO Announces Ambitious Plans for Venus Mission 'Shukrayaan-1' by 2028
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has officially unveiled its detailed roadmap for 'Shukrayaan-1', India's first mission to Venus.
• The mission aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface, focusing on its geological and atmospheric evolution.
• Shukrayaan-1 will carry a suite of indigenous scientific instruments developed by various Indian research institutions.
• The launch is tentatively scheduled for 2028, leveraging optimal orbital mechanics for the journey to Venus.
• This ambitious project underscores India's growing capabilities in planetary exploration and its commitment to advancing space science.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is the primary space agency of India.
Venus is the second planet from the Sun, known for its extremely hot and dense atmosphere.
Previous Indian planetary missions include Chandrayaan (Moon) and Mangalyaan (Mars).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of planetary exploration missions like Shukrayaan-1 for scientific understanding and technological advancement.
Analyze India's journey in space exploration, highlighting key achievements and its vision for future missions.
Evaluate the role of indigenous technology in making India's space program cost-effective and self-reliant.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits derived from advancements in space science and technology.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus, with its dense atmosphere visible.
articles.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to their similar size and mass, but it has vastly different atmospheric conditions.

articles.headline: Government Launches National Climate Adaptation Fund to Bolster Resilience Efforts
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has officially launched the National Climate Adaptation Fund (NCAF) with an initial corpus to support climate resilience projects across the country.
• The fund aims to finance initiatives focused on climate change adaptation, such as drought-proofing, flood management, coastal protection, and resilient agriculture.
• States and Union Territories can access the fund for implementing projects aligned with their State Action Plans on Climate Change.
• This move signifies India's commitment to addressing the impacts of climate change at a grassroots level and building adaptive capacities.
• The NCAF is expected to play a crucial role in mainstreaming climate adaptation into development planning and investment.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
keyTerms: State Action Plans on Climate Change (SAPCC)
prelimsPoints: The NCAF is a government initiative to support climate adaptation.
It focuses on projects like drought-proofing and flood management.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement on climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing vulnerability of India to climate change impacts and the necessity of robust adaptation strategies.
Analyze the objectives and potential effectiveness of the National Climate Adaptation Fund in building climate resilience across diverse regions of India.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing climate adaptation projects at the state level and suggest measures for effective utilization of such funds.
Examine how climate adaptation efforts can be integrated with broader sustainable development goals and local livelihoods.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various climate adaptation measures like resilient crops, water conservation, and flood barriers.
articles.trivia: India's National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) outlines eight missions to address climate change.

articles.headline: New National Education Policy Reforms Focus on Skill Development and Vocational Training
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced a series of reforms under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, specifically targeting skill development and vocational training.
• The reforms aim to integrate vocational education from the school level, making it accessible and flexible for students across various disciplines.
• Key initiatives include establishing more skill hubs, industry partnerships, and credit-based vocational courses to enhance employability.
• The policy emphasizes a multidisciplinary approach, allowing students to combine academic studies with practical skills.
• These changes are designed to bridge the skill gap in the workforce and align education with the demands of the 21st-century economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
The policy aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Vocational education is a key component of the NEP's vision for holistic development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives of the National Education Policy 2020 with a special focus on its provisions for skill development and vocational education.
Discuss the challenges in integrating vocational training effectively into the mainstream education system in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of these reforms on India's demographic dividend and workforce employability.
Suggest measures to strengthen industry-academia linkages to ensure that vocational training programs are relevant and demand-driven.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in practical vocational training, such as electronics or carpentry, in a modern classroom setting.
articles.trivia: The NEP 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.

articles.headline: DRDO Successfully Conducts Test Flight of Advanced Hypersonic Missile
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) announced the successful test flight of an indigenously developed advanced hypersonic missile.
• The missile demonstrated exceptional speed and maneuverability, reaching speeds significantly exceeding Mach 5 during its test trajectory.
• This achievement places India among a select group of nations possessing advanced hypersonic technology, crucial for future warfare.
• The test validated key technological parameters, including the hypersonic propulsion system and advanced guidance capabilities.
• This development significantly boosts India's strategic deterrence capabilities and reinforces its 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (self-reliant India) initiative in defence.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Hypersonic missiles travel at speeds greater than Mach 5 (five times the speed of sound).
DRDO is India's premier agency for military research and development.
India is working on both hypersonic cruise missiles and hypersonic glide vehicles.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic implications of India developing hypersonic missile technology for its regional and global security posture.
Analyze the role of DRDO in fostering indigenous defence capabilities and contributing to the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' vision.
Evaluate the technological challenges involved in developing hypersonic weapons and how India is addressing them.
Examine the impact of emerging military technologies like hypersonic missiles on the future of warfare and international arms control.
articles.imageDescription: A sleek, advanced missile launching into the sky, leaving a trail of smoke, symbolizing technological prowess.
articles.trivia: The speed of sound, Mach 1, is approximately 1,225 kilometers per hour (761 mph) at sea level.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Key Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
articles.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) has submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Bill, 2023.
• The recommendations focus on strengthening data fiduciary accountability, enhancing data principal rights, and clarifying cross-border data transfer mechanisms.
• Concerns were raised regarding the 'deemed consent' provision and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The committee emphasized the need for a balanced approach that protects individual privacy while fostering innovation in the digital economy.
• The proposed amendments are expected to be incorporated before the bill is tabled for final parliamentary approval.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Basics of cyber security
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Bill aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) is formed by members from both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
The concept of 'right to privacy' was affirmed as a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical issues and challenges in drafting a robust data protection law in India, considering the balance between privacy and innovation.
Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill and the significance of the parliamentary committee's recommendations.
Evaluate the potential impact of the DPDP Bill on individuals' privacy rights and the operations of digital businesses in India.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's proposed law aligns with or deviates from them.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of data flowing across a network, with a shield icon representing data protection.
articles.trivia: The first draft of a data protection bill in India was proposed by the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee in 2018.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Incentive Scheme for Green Hydrogen Production
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government has unveiled a new production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme aimed at boosting green hydrogen production in India.
• The scheme provides financial incentives for manufacturers of electrolyzers and producers of green hydrogen, promoting indigenous capacities.
• This initiative is a crucial step towards achieving India's ambitious National Green Hydrogen Mission targets and reducing carbon emissions.
• It is expected to attract significant investment in renewable energy and green technology sectors, creating new jobs.
• India aims to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and climate goals.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation.
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy through electrolysis of water.
The PLI scheme is a government initiative to boost domestic manufacturing.
India aims to achieve net-zero carbon emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate change mitigation goals.
Analyze the key features and expected impact of the new incentive scheme on green hydrogen production and adoption in India.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in developing a robust green hydrogen ecosystem, including infrastructure and cost-effectiveness.
Examine how India can leverage its renewable energy potential to become a global leader in green hydrogen production and export.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized image of a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe.

articles.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global South Climate Initiatives at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• India's representative at a key United Nations climate forum reiterated the nation's commitment to supporting climate initiatives in the Global South.
• The pledge includes increased financial assistance, technology transfer, and capacity building for developing nations disproportionately affected by climate change.
• India emphasized the principle of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in global climate action.
• The statement highlighted India's role as a voice for developing countries, advocating for equitable climate finance and accessible green technologies.
• This reaffirms India's leadership in South-South cooperation and its efforts to build a united front on climate justice issues.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: The concept of 'Global South' refers to developing countries, often in the Southern Hemisphere.
CBDR-RC is a principle enshrined in international environmental law.
The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) is the primary international forum for climate action.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the 'Global South' in contemporary international relations and India's role in championing its interests.
Analyze the principle of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations.
Evaluate India's efforts in promoting South-South cooperation, particularly in addressing climate change challenges.
Examine the challenges faced by developing countries in accessing climate finance and technology, and suggest mechanisms for equitable distribution.
articles.imageDescription: Delegates from various nations seated at a UN climate conference, with country flags visible.
articles.trivia: The first Earth Summit, held in Rio de Janeiro in 1992, established the framework for international climate action.

articles.headline: Health Ministry Launches Pan-India Campaign Against Non-Communicable Diseases
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry has rolled out a comprehensive pan-India campaign focused on prevention, early detection, and management of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign targets major NCDs such as cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, cancer, and chronic respiratory diseases, which account for a significant burden of illness.
• Key components include awareness drives, lifestyle modification programs, screening camps, and improved access to diagnostics and treatment at primary healthcare centers.
• The initiative emphasizes community participation and inter-sectoral coordination to promote healthy living and reduce NCD prevalence.
• This aligns with India's commitment to achieving Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 3.4, which aims to reduce premature mortality from NCDs by one-third by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
articles.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: NCDs are chronic diseases that are not transmitted from person to person.
SDG 3 aims to 'Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages'.
Ayushman Bharat Yojana is a major government healthcare scheme.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and their socio-economic implications.
Analyze the key strategies and challenges in implementing a pan-India campaign for NCD prevention and control.
Evaluate the role of primary healthcare infrastructure and community health workers in addressing the NCD challenge at the grassroots level.
Examine how lifestyle factors, diet, and environmental determinants contribute to NCDs and suggest policy interventions for promoting healthier populations.
articles.imageDescription: A poster showing healthy lifestyle choices like exercise, balanced diet, and regular health check-ups.
articles.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the significant discovery of an ancient Buddhist monastery complex in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation has revealed several stupas, prayer halls, monastic cells, and intricate Buddhist sculptures dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This find provides crucial insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its architectural and artistic traditions.
• The site is believed to be part of a larger Buddhist circuit in Odisha, which includes famous sites like Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, and Udayagiri.
• Efforts are underway for further exploration and conservation of the newly discovered heritage site, enhancing India's cultural tourism potential.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: The ASI is responsible for archaeological research and conservation of cultural heritage in India.
Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage, particularly in its 'Diamond Triangle' of Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, and Udayagiri.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significant contributions of Buddhism to India's art, architecture, and philosophical traditions.
Analyze the importance of archaeological discoveries like the one in Odisha for reconstructing ancient Indian history and cultural linkages.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in the preservation and management of India's vast cultural heritage sites.
Examine the potential of cultural tourism in promoting local economies and fostering a deeper appreciation for India's heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery, showing stone foundations and sculpted figures.
articles.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, Udayagiri) is known for its extensive Buddhist ruins.

articles.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Water Treaty Provisions Amid Changing Hydrological Realities
articles.explainer:
• India has formally initiated discussions for a review of certain provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing changing hydrological realities.
• The call for review comes amidst increasing water scarcity and the need for optimal utilization of shared river waters for irrigation and power generation.
• India emphasized the need for a more contemporary interpretation of the treaty's clauses to address modern challenges like climate change and new project requirements.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River System between India and Pakistan.
• This move could lead to complex negotiations but highlights India's proactive stance on water resource management and bilateral relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: The Indus Water Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960.
It allocates the waters of the Beas, Ravi, and Sutlej rivers to India, and the Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab rivers to Pakistan.
The World Bank acted as a guarantor for the treaty.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the reasons behind India's call for a review of the IWT, particularly in light of changing hydrological patterns and increasing water demands.
Evaluate the potential challenges and opportunities in renegotiating or reinterpreting international water treaties amidst geopolitical complexities.
Examine the broader implications of water scarcity and transboundary water disputes for regional stability and cooperation in South Asia.
articles.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and the allocation of rivers between India and Pakistan as per the IWT.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties globally, having survived several conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: Study Reveals Intensifying Urban Heat Island Effect in Major Indian Cities
articles.explainer:
• A new study published by environmental scientists highlights a significant intensification of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in several major Indian cities.
• The research indicates that urban areas are experiencing substantially higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas, particularly during nighttime.
• Factors contributing to this effect include dense infrastructure, lack of green spaces, heat-absorbing materials, and anthropogenic heat emissions.
• The UHI effect poses severe public health risks, increases energy consumption for cooling, and exacerbates air pollution challenges.
• The study calls for urgent urban planning interventions, including promoting green infrastructure, cool roofs, and sustainable building materials, to mitigate the impact.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect is a phenomenon where urban areas are warmer than surrounding rural areas.
Green spaces and water bodies can help mitigate the UHI effect.
Air pollution can be exacerbated by higher temperatures due to chemical reactions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the causes and consequences of the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect in rapidly urbanizing Indian cities.
Analyze the public health implications and increased energy demands associated with intensified UHI effects.
Evaluate various urban planning and architectural strategies that can be adopted to mitigate the UHI effect and promote urban climate resilience.
Examine the role of local governance and citizen participation in implementing sustainable urban development practices to combat environmental challenges.
articles.imageDescription: A satellite thermal image of a city showing warmer red areas in the urban core and cooler blue areas in surrounding rural regions.
articles.trivia: The UHI effect can increase a city's air temperature by as much as 10°C compared to nearby rural areas.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian herpetologists has announced the discovery of a new species of frog in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The newly identified amphibian, yet to be formally named, exhibits unique morphological and genetic characteristics, distinguishing it from known species.
• This discovery underscores the immense, yet underexplored, biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
• Habitat loss and climate change continue to pose significant threats to the region's endemic species, making such discoveries crucial for conservation efforts.
• The findings highlight the need for enhanced research, protection, and sustainable management practices in India's biodiversity hotspots.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Amphibians are vertebrates that typically live both on land and in water.
UNESCO World Heritage Sites are places of outstanding universal value.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for understanding ecosystem health and informing conservation strategies.
Evaluate the challenges in protecting endemic species in biodiversity-rich regions and suggest integrated approaches for conservation and sustainable development.
Examine the role of scientific research and public awareness in promoting biodiversity conservation in India.
articles.imageDescription: A small, colorful frog with distinct markings, perched on a leaf in a lush green forest.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

highlights: Eight police officers and one civilian killed in a major Naxal landmine attack in Bijapur, Chhattisgarh, raising internal security concerns.
Indian Coast Guard HAL Dhruv helicopter crashes during training in Porbandar, Gujarat, claiming the lives of all three crew members.
Nine miners tragically killed in a coal mine flooding incident in Dima Hasao, Assam, highlighting dangers of illegal mining.
Eminent Kannada novelist Na D'Souza, recipient of the Sahitya Akademi Award, passes away at 87.
Supreme Court reaffirms stringent environmental clearance norms for infrastructure projects, emphasizing sustainable development.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Naxal attack in Bijapur, consider the following statements:
1. Bijapur district is located in the state of Chhattisgarh.
2. Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) primarily affects only the states of Chhattisgarh and Jharkhand.
3. Landmines are a common tactic used by Naxalites.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Bijapur district is indeed a part of Chhattisgarh, a state significantly affected by LWE. Statement 2 is incorrect: While Chhattisgarh and Jharkhand are highly affected, LWE also impacts other states like Odisha, Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, and parts of Bihar and Telangana. Statement 3 is correct: Landmines and IEDs (Improvised Explosive Devices) are frequently used by Naxalites in ambushes against security forces.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the HAL Dhruv helicopter is/are correct?
1. It is an indigenously developed Advanced Light Helicopter (ALH).
2. It is primarily used by the Indian Air Force and Navy, but not the Coast Guard.
3. Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) is its manufacturer.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: HAL Dhruv is an indigenously developed Advanced Light Helicopter. Statement 2 is incorrect: The HAL Dhruv is widely used by the Indian Air Force, Army, Navy, and the Indian Coast Guard. Statement 3 is correct: It is manufactured by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL), a public sector aerospace and defence company.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect:
1. It refers to urban areas being significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas.
2. Dense infrastructure and lack of green spaces contribute to this phenomenon.
3. The UHI effect typically reduces energy consumption for cooling in urban areas.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect describes how urban areas experience higher temperatures than nearby rural areas. Statement 2 is correct: Factors like impervious surfaces (concrete, asphalt), lack of vegetation, and anthropogenic heat sources contribute to UHI. Statement 3 is incorrect: The UHI effect increases temperatures, thus *increasing* the demand for cooling, leading to higher energy consumption.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to India's space program, what is 'Shukrayaan-1'?
options: (a) India's first mission to study black holes.
(b) An upcoming Indian mission to Venus.
(c) A new satellite for earth observation and disaster management.
(d) India's proposed mission to the asteroid belt.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) An upcoming Indian mission to Venus.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Shukrayaan-1 is ISRO's ambitious first mission to Venus, aimed at studying its atmosphere, surface, and subsurface. It is tentatively scheduled for launch in 2028.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following principles is often invoked by India in international climate change negotiations to highlight historical responsibilities of developed nations?
options: (b) Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The principle of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) acknowledges that all states have a shared responsibility to address climate change (common), but they have different capacities and historical responsibilities (differentiated). India frequently invokes this principle to advocate for developed nations to take greater responsibility and provide financial and technological support to developing countries.

mainsMocks.question: The recent Naxal attack in Bijapur underscores the persistent challenges in tackling Left-Wing Extremism (LWE) in India. Analyze the multi-faceted approach adopted by the government to counter LWE and critically evaluate its effectiveness, suggesting further measures for comprehensive resolution. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism. Various security forces and agencies and their mandate.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the Bijapur attack and the LWE challenge. Discuss the government's two-pronged strategy: security operations and development initiatives. Analyze the effectiveness of these measures (e.g., reduction in geographical spread, but persistent violence in core areas; success of some development schemes, but issues with implementation). Suggest further measures like intelligence strengthening, community policing, targeted development, land reforms, and addressing root causes.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's reaffirmation of stringent environmental clearance norms highlights the delicate balance between development and environmental protection. Discuss the constitutional provisions and legal frameworks that govern environmental protection in India. In light of the ruling, suggest how India can achieve sustainable development without compromising its ecological integrity. (15 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation. GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Start with the SC ruling's significance. Detail constitutional provisions (Articles 21, 48A, 51A(g)) and key environmental laws (Environment Protection Act, EIA Notification). Analyze the challenges in balancing development with environmental protection. Suggest measures for sustainable development: green infrastructure, renewable energy, circular economy, public participation in EIA, robust monitoring, technological solutions, and environmental education.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambitious National Green Hydrogen Mission and the new incentive scheme for green hydrogen production signify a major step towards energy security and decarbonization. Discuss the potential of green hydrogen as a future energy source for India. What challenges need to be addressed to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export? (10 Marks)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment. Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc. Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce green hydrogen and India's mission. Explain its potential: energy security, reduced emissions, new industries, export opportunities. Discuss challenges: high production cost (electrolyzers, renewable energy), infrastructure (storage, transport), technological advancements, water availability, policy stability, and international competition. Suggest solutions: R&D, PLI schemes, international collaborations, green financing, and skill development.

quickBytes.text: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha refers to the Buddhist sites of Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, and Udayagiri.
The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has previously banned 'rat-hole mining' due to its environmental and safety hazards.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' holds that those who produce pollution should bear the costs of managing it.
India aims to achieve Net-Zero carbon emissions by the year 2070.
The Indus Water Treaty (IWT) was brokered by the World Bank between India and Pakistan in 1960.
The 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative aims to make India a self-reliant nation across various sectors.
SDG 3.4 aims to reduce premature mortality from non-communicable diseases by one-third by 2030.
Mach 5 refers to five times the speed of sound, a benchmark for hypersonic technology.
The Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.

GS1.headline: Eminent Kannada Novelist Na D'Souza Passes Away at 87
GS1.explainer:
• Noted Kannada novelist and writer Na D'Souza, known for his significant contributions to children's literature and social themes, passed away at the age of 87.
• D'Souza was a recipient of numerous awards, including the Sahitya Akademi Award for his overall contribution to Kannada literature.
• His works often explored themes of human relationships, rural life, and the challenges faced by marginalized communities.
• He was particularly celebrated for his novel 'Dweepa' (The Island), which was adapted into a critically acclaimed film.
• His demise marks a significant loss for Kannada literature and the broader Indian literary landscape.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Na D'Souza was a prominent figure in Kannada literature.
He was known for his novel 'Dweepa'.
The Sahitya Akademi Award is a literary honour in India given by the Sahitya Akademi, India's National Academy of Letters.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of regional literature in reflecting and shaping societal values and cultural identity in India.
Analyze the contributions of writers like Na D'Souza in promoting social awareness and addressing contemporary issues through their literary works.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for regional languages and literature in the digital age.
GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of Na D'Souza, an elderly man with a thoughtful expression.
GS1.trivia: Na D'Souza also served as a journalist and worked for various Kannada newspapers and magazines.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Ancient Buddhist Monastery in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) has announced the significant discovery of an ancient Buddhist monastery complex in the Jajpur district of Odisha.
• The excavation has revealed several stupas, prayer halls, monastic cells, and intricate Buddhist sculptures dating back to the 7th-10th centuries CE.
• This find provides crucial insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India and its architectural and artistic traditions.
• The site is believed to be part of a larger Buddhist circuit in Odisha, which includes famous sites like Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, and Udayagiri.
• Efforts are underway for further exploration and conservation of the newly discovered heritage site, enhancing India's cultural tourism potential.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: The ASI is responsible for archaeological research and conservation of cultural heritage in India.
Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage, particularly in its 'Diamond Triangle' of Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, and Udayagiri.
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significant contributions of Buddhism to India's art, architecture, and philosophical traditions.
Analyze the importance of archaeological discoveries like the one in Odisha for reconstructing ancient Indian history and cultural linkages.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) in the preservation and management of India's vast cultural heritage sites.
Examine the potential of cultural tourism in promoting local economies and fostering a deeper appreciation for India's heritage.
GS1.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery, showing stone foundations and sculpted figures.
GS1.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri, Udayagiri) is known for its extensive Buddhist ruins.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Stringent Environmental Clearance Norms for Infrastructure Projects
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today reaffirmed the necessity of stringent environmental clearance (EC) norms for all major infrastructure projects.
• The ruling came in a batch of petitions challenging relaxations in EC processes, emphasizing the 'polluter pays' principle and sustainable development.
• The court highlighted the irreversible damage caused by unchecked development and the importance of public participation in environmental impact assessments (EIAs).
• This decision is expected to have significant implications for balancing economic growth with environmental protection.
• It reinforces the judiciary's role in safeguarding ecological balance and holding project proponents accountable.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: Environmental Clearance (EC) is mandated under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
The 'Polluter Pays Principle' is a key concept in environmental law.
EIA is a tool used to assess the environmental consequences of a plan, policy, program, or project.
mainsPoints: Analyze the conflict between economic development and environmental conservation in India, citing relevant Supreme Court judgments.
Discuss the strengths and weaknesses of India's current environmental clearance process and suggest reforms for more effective implementation.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding environmental justice and promoting sustainable development goals in India.
Examine how public participation can be strengthened in the EIA process to ensure greater transparency and accountability.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi, symbolizing judicial authority.
GS2.trivia: India's first major environmental legislation was the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974.

GS2.headline: India Proposes New Framework for Indo-Pacific Economic Cooperation at Regional Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India has put forth a comprehensive proposal for an enhanced Indo-Pacific Economic Cooperation Framework during a virtual regional summit.
• The framework aims to foster greater trade, investment, supply chain resilience, and digital economy collaboration among member nations.
• The proposal emphasizes principles of inclusivity, transparency, and respect for sovereignty, positioning India as a key player in regional architecture.
• Discussions focused on critical areas such as clean energy transition, infrastructure development, and maritime security.
• This initiative underscores India's growing strategic interest and leadership role in shaping the economic future of the Indo-Pacific region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The Indo-Pacific region is strategically important for global trade and security.
India is a key member of the Quad (Quadrilateral Security Dialogue) which focuses on the Indo-Pacific.
Supply chain resilience became a critical issue during the COVID-19 pandemic.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's strategic rationale behind actively engaging in Indo-Pacific economic and security frameworks.
Discuss the potential benefits and challenges for India in promoting a new economic cooperation framework in the Indo-Pacific region.
Evaluate how India's 'Act East' policy integrates with its broader Indo-Pacific strategy.
Examine the role of multilateral economic groupings in shaping regional trade and investment landscapes.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized map of the Indo-Pacific region, highlighting key trade routes and countries.
GS2.trivia: The term 'Indo-Pacific' gained prominence in strategic discourse in the early 21st century, emphasizing the interconnectedness of the Indian and Pacific Oceans.

GS2.headline: New National Education Policy Reforms Focus on Skill Development and Vocational Training
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education announced a series of reforms under the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, specifically targeting skill development and vocational training.
• The reforms aim to integrate vocational education from the school level, making it accessible and flexible for students across various disciplines.
• Key initiatives include establishing more skill hubs, industry partnerships, and credit-based vocational courses to enhance employability.
• The policy emphasizes a multidisciplinary approach, allowing students to combine academic studies with practical skills.
• These changes are designed to bridge the skill gap in the workforce and align education with the demands of the 21st-century economy.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 replaced the National Policy on Education of 1986.
The policy aims for universalization of education from pre-school to secondary level.
Vocational education is a key component of the NEP's vision for holistic development.
mainsPoints: Analyze the objectives of the National Education Policy 2020 with a special focus on its provisions for skill development and vocational education.
Discuss the challenges in integrating vocational training effectively into the mainstream education system in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of these reforms on India's demographic dividend and workforce employability.
Suggest measures to strengthen industry-academia linkages to ensure that vocational training programs are relevant and demand-driven.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in practical vocational training, such as electronics or carpentry, in a modern classroom setting.
GS2.trivia: The NEP 2020 aims to increase the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education to 50% by 2035.

GS2.headline: Parliamentary Committee Recommends Key Amendments to Digital Personal Data Protection Bill
GS2.explainer:
• A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) has submitted its report recommending significant amendments to the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Bill, 2023.
• The recommendations focus on strengthening data fiduciary accountability, enhancing data principal rights, and clarifying cross-border data transfer mechanisms.
• Concerns were raised regarding the 'deemed consent' provision and the independence of the Data Protection Board.
• The committee emphasized the need for a balanced approach that protects individual privacy while fostering innovation in the digital economy.
• The proposed amendments are expected to be incorporated before the bill is tabled for final parliamentary approval.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
Basics of cyber security
prelimsPoints: The DPDP Bill aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data.
A Joint Parliamentary Committee (JPC) is formed by members from both Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.
The concept of 'right to privacy' was affirmed as a fundamental right by the Supreme Court in the Puttaswamy judgment.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical issues and challenges in drafting a robust data protection law in India, considering the balance between privacy and innovation.
Analyze the key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Bill and the significance of the parliamentary committee's recommendations.
Evaluate the potential impact of the DPDP Bill on individuals' privacy rights and the operations of digital businesses in India.
Examine the global best practices in data protection and how India's proposed law aligns with or deviates from them.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized image of data flowing across a network, with a shield icon representing data protection.
GS2.trivia: The first draft of a data protection bill in India was proposed by the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee in 2018.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Enhanced Support for Global South Climate Initiatives at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• India's representative at a key United Nations climate forum reiterated the nation's commitment to supporting climate initiatives in the Global South.
• The pledge includes increased financial assistance, technology transfer, and capacity building for developing nations disproportionately affected by climate change.
• India emphasized the principle of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in global climate action.
• The statement highlighted India's role as a voice for developing countries, advocating for equitable climate finance and accessible green technologies.
• This reaffirms India's leadership in South-South cooperation and its efforts to build a united front on climate justice issues.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC)
prelimsPoints: The concept of 'Global South' refers to developing countries, often in the Southern Hemisphere.
CBDR-RC is a principle enshrined in international environmental law.
The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) is the primary international forum for climate action.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the 'Global South' in contemporary international relations and India's role in championing its interests.
Analyze the principle of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in the context of global climate negotiations.
Evaluate India's efforts in promoting South-South cooperation, particularly in addressing climate change challenges.
Examine the challenges faced by developing countries in accessing climate finance and technology, and suggest mechanisms for equitable distribution.
GS2.imageDescription: Delegates from various nations seated at a UN climate conference, with country flags visible.
GS2.trivia: The first Earth Summit, held in Rio de Janeiro in 1992, established the framework for international climate action.

GS2.headline: Health Ministry Launches Pan-India Campaign Against Non-Communicable Diseases
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry has rolled out a comprehensive pan-India campaign focused on prevention, early detection, and management of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs).
• The campaign targets major NCDs such as cardiovascular diseases, diabetes, cancer, and chronic respiratory diseases, which account for a significant burden of illness.
• Key components include awareness drives, lifestyle modification programs, screening camps, and improved access to diagnostics and treatment at primary healthcare centers.
• The initiative emphasizes community participation and inter-sectoral coordination to promote healthy living and reduce NCD prevalence.
• This aligns with India's commitment to achieving Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 3.4, which aims to reduce premature mortality from NCDs by one-third by 2030.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
GS2.source: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
prelimsPoints: NCDs are chronic diseases that are not transmitted from person to person.
SDG 3 aims to 'Ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages'.
Ayushman Bharat Yojana is a major government healthcare scheme.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing burden of Non-Communicable Diseases (NCDs) in India and their socio-economic implications.
Analyze the key strategies and challenges in implementing a pan-India campaign for NCD prevention and control.
Evaluate the role of primary healthcare infrastructure and community health workers in addressing the NCD challenge at the grassroots level.
Examine how lifestyle factors, diet, and environmental determinants contribute to NCDs and suggest policy interventions for promoting healthier populations.
GS2.imageDescription: A poster showing healthy lifestyle choices like exercise, balanced diet, and regular health check-ups.
GS2.trivia: Globally, NCDs kill 41 million people each year, equivalent to 74% of all deaths.

GS2.headline: India Calls for Review of Indus Water Treaty Provisions Amid Changing Hydrological Realities
GS2.explainer:
• India has formally initiated discussions for a review of certain provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing changing hydrological realities.
• The call for review comes amidst increasing water scarcity and the need for optimal utilization of shared river waters for irrigation and power generation.
• India emphasized the need for a more contemporary interpretation of the treaty's clauses to address modern challenges like climate change and new project requirements.
• The IWT, brokered by the World Bank in 1960, governs the sharing of waters of the Indus River System between India and Pakistan.
• This move could lead to complex negotiations but highlights India's proactive stance on water resource management and bilateral relations.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: The Indus Water Treaty (IWT) was signed in 1960.
It allocates the waters of the Beas, Ravi, and Sutlej rivers to India, and the Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab rivers to Pakistan.
The World Bank acted as a guarantor for the treaty.
mainsPoints: Analyze the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT) and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Discuss the reasons behind India's call for a review of the IWT, particularly in light of changing hydrological patterns and increasing water demands.
Evaluate the potential challenges and opportunities in renegotiating or reinterpreting international water treaties amidst geopolitical complexities.
Examine the broader implications of water scarcity and transboundary water disputes for regional stability and cooperation in South Asia.
GS2.imageDescription: A map showing the Indus River System and the allocation of rivers between India and Pakistan as per the IWT.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties globally, having survived several conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS3.headline: Eight Police Personnel Killed in Bijapur Naxal Attack, Raises Internal Security Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• A vehicle carrying police personnel was targeted by a landmine planted by Naxalites in Bijapur district, Chhattisgarh.
• Eight police officers and one civilian were killed in the ambush, highlighting the persistent challenge of Left-Wing Extremism (LWE).
• The incident occurred during a routine patrol, leading to a strong condemnation from central and state authorities.
• Security forces have launched a massive search operation in the region to apprehend the perpetrators.
• This attack underscores the need for enhanced intelligence, improved anti-Naxal strategies, and community engagement in affected areas.
gsSubTopics: Linkages between development and spread of extremism
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security
Challenges to internal security through communication networks
Role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas
Linkages of organized crime with terrorism
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Bijapur district is located in Chhattisgarh, a state significantly affected by LWE.
Landmines are a common tactic used by Naxalites in ambushes.
Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) like CRPF assist state police in anti-Naxal operations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the socio-economic factors contributing to the persistence of Naxalism in regions like Chhattisgarh.
Discuss the effectiveness of current anti-Naxal strategies, including development initiatives and security operations.
Evaluate the challenges faced by security forces in LWE-affected areas and suggest measures for improving their operational capabilities and intelligence gathering.
Examine the role of state and central governments in coordinating efforts to tackle Left-Wing Extremism.
GS3.imageDescription: Security personnel inspecting the site of a Naxal attack in a forested area of Bijapur, Chhattisgarh.
GS3.trivia: The term 'Naxalite' originated from Naxalbari village in West Bengal, where a peasant uprising took place in 1967.

GS3.headline: Indian Coast Guard HAL Dhruv Helicopter Crashes in Porbandar, Three Crew Members Dead
GS3.explainer:
• An advanced light helicopter (ALH) Dhruv of the Indian Coast Guard crashed during a training flight near Porbandar, Gujarat.
• All three crew members on board, including the pilot, co-pilot, and an observer, succumbed to their injuries.
• A court of inquiry has been ordered to investigate the cause of the accident, which occurred during a routine sortie.
• The HAL Dhruv is an indigenously developed utility helicopter, widely used by the Indian Armed Forces and Coast Guard.
• The incident raises concerns about aviation safety protocols and maintenance procedures for military aircraft.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate
Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
prelimsPoints: HAL Dhruv is manufactured by Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).
The Indian Coast Guard is a multi-mission organization conducting maritime law enforcement and search and rescue operations.
Porbandar is a coastal city in Gujarat.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of indigenization in India's defence sector and the role of HAL in achieving self-reliance.
Analyze the challenges in maintaining aviation safety standards for military aircraft in India and suggest measures for improvement.
Examine the role and responsibilities of the Indian Coast Guard in ensuring maritime security and safety along India's coastline.
GS3.imageDescription: An Indian Coast Guard HAL Dhruv helicopter in flight over a coastal area.
GS3.trivia: The HAL Dhruv has been exported to several countries, including Ecuador, Maldives, and Mauritius.

GS3.headline: Nine Miners Trapped and Killed in Dima Hasao Coal Mine Flooding, Assam
GS3.explainer:
• A tragic incident unfolded in Dima Hasao district, Assam, where nine miners were killed due to flooding in an illegal coal mine.
• The miners were reportedly trapped after heavy rains caused a sudden influx of water into the unscientific 'rat-hole' mine.
• Rescue operations faced significant challenges due to the remote location, hazardous conditions, and lack of proper mine safety equipment.
• The incident highlights the pervasive issue of illegal mining in resource-rich regions, often leading to environmental degradation and loss of life.
• Authorities have initiated an inquiry into the incident and pledged action against those responsible for operating the illegal mine.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation
Environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: energy, ports, roads, airports, railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: Dima Hasao district is located in Assam, known for its hilly terrain and mineral resources.
Rat-hole mining is a controversial and dangerous method of coal extraction.
The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has previously banned rat-hole mining due to its environmental and safety hazards.
mainsPoints: Discuss the socio-economic factors that drive illegal mining activities in India, particularly in states like Assam.
Analyze the environmental consequences of unscientific mining practices, including land degradation, water pollution, and biodiversity loss.
Evaluate the challenges in enforcing mining regulations and ensuring worker safety in remote and LWE-affected areas.
Suggest comprehensive measures to curb illegal mining, including technological interventions, community involvement, and stringent legal frameworks.
GS3.imageDescription: Rescue workers attempting to pump water out of a flooded mine shaft in a remote, hilly area.
GS3.trivia: Rat-hole mining involves digging narrow tunnels, typically only large enough for one person to enter, to extract coal.

GS3.headline: India's Q3 Economic Growth Projections Remain Robust Amid Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• Several leading economic agencies and the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) have released their projections for India's Q3 (October-December) economic growth, indicating continued robustness.
• Despite global economic slowdowns and geopolitical uncertainties, India's domestic consumption and investment are expected to drive growth.
• Manufacturing and services sectors show signs of resilience, contributing significantly to the GDP figures.
• Inflation remains a concern, but the RBI's monetary policy stance is seen as balancing growth and price stability.
• The government's focus on capital expenditure and structural reforms is credited with providing a stable foundation for economic expansion.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: Q3 in the Indian financial year refers to October-December.
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is responsible for monetary policy in India.
GDP growth is a key indicator of economic health.
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers of India's economic growth in recent quarters and identify potential challenges to sustaining this momentum.
Discuss the role of the Reserve Bank of India's monetary policy in managing inflation and supporting economic growth.
Evaluate the impact of government's capital expenditure and structural reforms on India's economic resilience amidst global headwinds.
Examine the concept of 'inclusive growth' in the context of India's economic expansion and suggest measures to achieve it.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing an upward trending line representing India's economic growth on a stock market-like chart.
GS3.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2027.

GS3.headline: ISRO Announces Ambitious Plans for Venus Mission 'Shukrayaan-1' by 2028
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) has officially unveiled its detailed roadmap for 'Shukrayaan-1', India's first mission to Venus.
• The mission aims to study Venus's atmosphere, surface, and subsurface, focusing on its geological and atmospheric evolution.
• Shukrayaan-1 will carry a suite of indigenous scientific instruments developed by various Indian research institutions.
• The launch is tentatively scheduled for 2028, leveraging optimal orbital mechanics for the journey to Venus.
• This ambitious project underscores India's growing capabilities in planetary exploration and its commitment to advancing space science.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology
Indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: ISRO is the primary space agency of India.
Venus is the second planet from the Sun, known for its extremely hot and dense atmosphere.
Previous Indian planetary missions include Chandrayaan (Moon) and Mangalyaan (Mars).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of planetary exploration missions like Shukrayaan-1 for scientific understanding and technological advancement.
Analyze India's journey in space exploration, highlighting key achievements and its vision for future missions.
Evaluate the role of indigenous technology in making India's space program cost-effective and self-reliant.
Examine the potential socio-economic benefits derived from advancements in space science and technology.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendering of the Shukrayaan-1 spacecraft orbiting Venus, with its dense atmosphere visible.
GS3.trivia: Venus is often called Earth's 'sister planet' due to their similar size and mass, but it has vastly different atmospheric conditions.

GS3.headline: Government Launches National Climate Adaptation Fund to Bolster Resilience Efforts
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government has officially launched the National Climate Adaptation Fund (NCAF) with an initial corpus to support climate resilience projects across the country.
• The fund aims to finance initiatives focused on climate change adaptation, such as drought-proofing, flood management, coastal protection, and resilient agriculture.
• States and Union Territories can access the fund for implementing projects aligned with their State Action Plans on Climate Change.
• This move signifies India's commitment to addressing the impacts of climate change at a grassroots level and building adaptive capacities.
• The NCAF is expected to play a crucial role in mainstreaming climate adaptation into development planning and investment.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
GS3.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
keyTerms: State Action Plans on Climate Change (SAPCC)
prelimsPoints: The NCAF is a government initiative to support climate adaptation.
It focuses on projects like drought-proofing and flood management.
India is a signatory to the Paris Agreement on climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing vulnerability of India to climate change impacts and the necessity of robust adaptation strategies.
Analyze the objectives and potential effectiveness of the National Climate Adaptation Fund in building climate resilience across diverse regions of India.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing climate adaptation projects at the state level and suggest measures for effective utilization of such funds.
Examine how climate adaptation efforts can be integrated with broader sustainable development goals and local livelihoods.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting various climate adaptation measures like resilient crops, water conservation, and flood barriers.
GS3.trivia: India's National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) outlines eight missions to address climate change.

GS3.headline: DRDO Successfully Conducts Test Flight of Advanced Hypersonic Missile
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) announced the successful test flight of an indigenously developed advanced hypersonic missile.
• The missile demonstrated exceptional speed and maneuverability, reaching speeds significantly exceeding Mach 5 during its test trajectory.
